meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek gold rush communities, mining pressure, roads, immigrants, and regional work.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Long Wintu presence around Clear Creek and the need to treat land memory as active context.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: County-seat, courthouse, mining-commerce, brick-and-timber material for northern California frontier scenes.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the prologue page that teaches the packet tone and opening situation without resolving the campaign. Do not write the finished prologue inside this JSON.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented road-book wording generated later
two compact paragraphs establishing the first trouble as a playable frontier situation
short bullet list of known pressures generated later
short bullet list of hidden pressures generated later
H3 First Night In French Gulch
generation_guidance: Open with a concrete situation suitable for session one: weather, money, law, rumor, and a road that will not stay open.
Keep secrets as prompts for play rather than fixed plot revelations.
Make ordinary objects carry pressure: wet payroll paper, a torn freight tag, a saddle cinch cut too clean, a horse that refuses a certain bridge.
Use French Gulch as the social room where everyone hears a different version before the posse rides.
Avoid naming final NPCs in this prompt; leave names, motives, and exact guilt for the future page generation.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: This page should feel hand-folded into a coat pocket by someone who meant to leave town before breakfast. It is the bad errand, not the grand legend.
generation_prompt.table_spark: Use the first night to make the table care about mud, witnesses, hunger, and who gets believed when the same road tells two stories.
must_preserve: French Gulch is the opening settlement.
The prologue creates playable pressure but does not solve the mystery.
The page must include the image reference path exactly: images/prologue.jpg.
The future prose should leave room for Native families, workers, law, wildlife, and company money as active forces.

style_constraints.era: Everything generated from this prompt belongs to 1899: horses, ore wagons, soot-black lamps, telegraph where appropriate, hand tools, rifles, ledgers, canvas, iron, mud, pine, and courthouse paper.
style_constraints.rdr_clone: Aim at an RDR-clone western tabletop packet: moral pressure, travel friction, camp routines, hunting, law heat, debt, reputation, and open-world consequence without copying named characters, missions, towns, logos, or slogans.
style_constraints.voice: Use a weathered oral-history cadence with practical table utility. The page should sound like it came from people who have slept outside, hauled ore, read tracks, and argued over land.
style_constraints.native_representation: Native American and tribal material must be grounded, specific, and respectful: living families, trade, land rights, stewardship, survival, diplomacy, and conflict. Do not invent sacred rites, fake language, costumes, or mystical tropes.
style_constraints.shape_priority: Follow the requested section order and heading levels exactly while inventing fresh 1899 western language.

avoid: do not write finished page prose inside this prompt file
do not include completed lore paragraphs, sample quotations, dialogue, final move text, or final rule text
do not mention any source packet, template packet, reference project, or inherited taxonomy
do not use copied names or terms from any prior non-western packet
do not include engine, hardware, runtime, profiling, build, deployment, or implementation details
do not include modern slang, modern technology, theme-park nostalgia, fake tribal language, or sacred ceremony invention
do not use placeholder filler such as TBD, lorem ipsum, or generic western flavor without a playable purpose
self_check: The generated markdown follows the required heading order exactly.
This JSON contains guidance only and no finished prologue prose.
The generated page creates a playable first pressure without locking the campaign into one answer.
The future page stays in grounded 1899 Jefferson State language.
No copied terms, inherited non-western vocabulary, modern props, or reference/source project names appear.
